target_statement: Republic of the Congo remains a net carbon sink and aims to strengthen this positive contribution to the global climate by 2035. The unconditional scenario takes into account current low-carbon measures in the sectors and those for which the country does not expect external support for their execution. Thanks to these efforts, the country progressively strengthens its performance to -21,717 ktCO2eq in 2030, representing an improvement of 7.8% compared to the [business as usual (BAU)] scenario of the same year. By 2035, removals reach -21,932 ktCO2eq, representing a 22% improvement compared to BAU, reflecting the consolidation of the country's role as a carbon sink through already operational programs. By adding additional measures that require external support (Combined Unconditional + Conditional scenario), net removals increase from -22,033 ktCO2eq in 2025 to -24,254 ktCO2eq in 2030, representing an improvement of 20.4% compared to BAU. By 2035, removals reach -26,569 ktCO2eq, representing an increase of 47.8% compared to BAU.

ndc_graph_notes: Iceland's NDC has two base years: (1) 2005 for gross emissions (excluding stationary installations under Directive 2003/87/EC and CO2 from civil aviation); and (2) 2025 for LULUCF emissions. LULUCF accounts for the majority (63%) of Iceland's emissions. In its NDC, Iceland's government projects LULUCF emissions will fall to 6.26 Mt CO2e in 2025. This chart approximates a 2025 base value for total net emissions by assuming that 2025 gross emissions will be on a roughly linear track from their 2005 base value to Iceland's updated 2030 NDC target. Projected gross emissions in 2025 are then added to projected LULUCF emissions. 2035 target is for net emissions including LULUCF but its 2030 target excludes LULUCF (and so is not depicted in this chart).
target_statement: Iceland is committed to a 50–55 per cent emission reduction target by 2035 compared to 2005 under the scope of the EU’s Effort Sharing Regulation (ESR). This represents a significant progression from the corresponding 41 per cent emission reduction target under ESR by 2030 compared to 2005.... Iceland is committed to a target of 400–500 kt CO2...net reduction of greenhouse gas emissions from the LULUCF sector by 2035 compared to 2025.

ndc_graph_notes: Eswatini's baseline emissions levels for 2010 and 2022 on which its BAU trajectory for its 2023-35 were projected are shown as much lower in its NDC (submitted Nov 2025) than in its latest national inventory (submitted Dec 2025).
target_statement: "Eswatini’s new economy wide target, compared to the Business as Usual (BAU) scenario, aims to reduce its greenhouse emissions (GHG) by 2.24 MtCO₂eq in 2035. This is an increase of 115% from the 1.04 MtCO₂eq reductions by 2030 in NDC 2.0. Subject to national circumstances, Eswatini aims to mobilize domestic resources to abate 695 ktCO₂eq tonnes of GHG emissions (31% of the 2.24 million tonnes). The reduction of the remaining 1.55 MtCO₂eq tonnes (69% of the total) will be achieved through a combination of international support, including finance, investments, technology development and transfer, and capacity building."

ndc_graph_notes: Thailand's NDC target assumes net emissions of 287.2 Mt CO<sub>2<sub>e in its 2019 base year, per its BTR submitted to the UN in Dec 2024. The NDC document notes that 'GHG emissions in the base year (2019) are subject to updates, depending on the...recalculation of the GHG inventory.' Its updated inventory estimates submitted to the UN in Jun 2025 show 2019 net emissions as 259.6 Mt CO<sub>2<sub>e. The 2035 target value depicted in the chart has been revised downward to reflect the updated estimates of base year emissions. 2035 target is for net emissions including LULUCF but its 2030 target excludes LULUCF (and so is not depicted in this chart).
target_statement: "Thailand aims to reduce its net GHG emissions to 152 million tCO2eq by 2035, representing a 47% reduction (135.2 MtCO2eq) compared to 2019 levels. This target aligns with the 1.5-degree pathways and a goal of net-zero emissions by 2050."

ndc_graph_notes: Yemen isn't a party to Paris Agreement but has submitted a NDC 'vision and framework;' its final NDC is still under preparation.
target_statement: "Yemen’s climate ambition is guided by five strategic principles: (1) integration of climate and development across all planning processes; (2) an adaptation-first approach to safeguard livelihoods and stability; (3) building a low-carbon and climate-resilient economy that targets at least 35 % renewable energy by 2035; (4) promoting inclusivity and climate justice to ensure equitable benefits; and (5) mobilizing domestic and international finance through innovation and partnerships. Collectively, these principles shape a unified national effort to transition from crisis to resilience, aligning Yemen’s development trajectory with the Paris Agreement and the outcomes of the Global Stocktake."
